title: Advertising Material Distribution Services

narrative.executive_view: This is a pass for a broad roll-up thesis. Exact NAICS 541870 covers physical direct distribution or delivery of advertisements and samples, including door-to-door, windshield placement and retail handouts [S1]. AI can improve routing, reporting, quoting and administration, but it does not perform the core last-mile work. The remaining economic question is whether advertisers keep paying for a physical medium when digital alternatives and postal distribution options constrain price; that is a terminal-demand problem, not an AI-automation opportunity.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: AI assists the thin information layer: carrier-route targeting, route design, job quoting, proof-of-delivery exception handling, billing, payroll administration and client reporting. It cannot walk routes, hang pieces, deliver samples or staff an in-store demonstration, the activities Census uses to define this code [S1]. The supplied 5418 occupation proxy is severely agency-weighted, so its large residual is treated as field-distribution labor with a low automatable fraction. Print-industry AI evidence is used only as a broad workflow analogue, not as evidence of autonomous delivery [S6].
narrative.value_capture: Distribution is likely to pass through a large portion of the limited office savings. USPS offers Every Door Direct Mail to target neighborhoods, cities and ZIP Codes [S3], creating a published alternative for a material portion of physical-advertising demand; a local distributor also competes against rebidding and against digital ad budgets. Reliable last-mile execution and evidence of delivery can preserve some service margin, but neither is an AI moat. Therefore low distribution retention and low moat inputs reflect price discipline, not a claim that AI removes the physical workforce.
narrative.adoption_timing: Effective AI adoption is below 50% and likely slower than in direct-mail production because only a minority of the actual wage bill is addressable. The 2025 printing study documents AI moving into estimating, customer communication and workflow functions, but also fragmented implementation and significant organizational barriers [S6]. I use 20% current effective adoption and five years to 50% for the addressable operating layer. This measures adoption of tools in the operator, not automation of door-to-door or sampling labor.
narrative.consolidation_economics: The deterministic band count is small and fragile: it rests on a generic 15.65% margin despite the code's labor-intensive physical-delivery definition. No verified, NAICS-specific consolidator count or completed-transaction multiple was located in the reopened public sources. The only available small-business valuation anchor is a sold printing/SDE cohort, which is a weak unit and sector proxy [S4]. Generic owner succession evidence is not enough to convert a small, highly local field-labor market into a roll-up thesis. Buy only where routes, client contracts, crew compliance and delivery verification are already proven.
narrative.terminal_demand: Terminal demand is melting for a generalist advertising-material distributor. Marketing Mail volume fell from 80.9bn pieces in FY2016 to 56.8bn in FY2025 [S2], while the USPS OIG describes long-run electronic diversion toward lower-cost online alternatives [S2]. The physical channel is not zero: USPS reports nearly 3bn EDDM pieces in FY2025 [S5]. But that evidence supports persistence of a channel, not durable pricing or a growing pool of independent last-mile distributors; direct-distribution firms also depend on non-postal flyers and samples, so the exact melt rate is uncertain.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: This code has unusually high boundary and data risk. Census separates 541870 physical delivery from 541860 campaign/preparation, 541890 specialties and 32311 printing [S1], but the deterministic role mix is the broad 5418 advertising group. The wage-share result could overstate automation if the unlisted residual is even more field-labor intensive than assumed. Multiples are not direct evidence: the source reports owner earnings/SDE for printing businesses, not EBITDA for advertising-material distribution. Contractor classification, route-density economics, client concentration, proof-of-delivery liability and the share of in-store sampling must be verified before any deal is underwritten.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual
sources.what_it_supports: Exact 2022 scope for 541870 physical distribution/delivery, and its boundary with 541860 direct-mail advertising, printing and advertising specialties.

sources.title: USPS OIG: Analysis of Historical Mail Volume Trends
sources.what_it_supports: USPS OIG evidence that Marketing Mail volume fell 40% from FY2008 to FY2023 because of diversion to lower-cost online alternatives.

sources.title: USPS Every Door Direct Mail
sources.what_it_supports: USPS EDDM offers neighborhood, city and ZIP Code targeting through retail or business-mail entry, a physical-distribution alternative relevant to this industry.

sources.title: Paper Manufacturing & Printing Business Valuation Benchmarks
sources.what_it_supports: Sold small printing-business owner-earnings/SDE valuation data: 2.45x median and 2.57x average for 2021-25; used only as a disclosed proxy, not as direct 541870 EBITDA evidence.

sources.title: USPS Postal Facts: Every Door Direct Mail
sources.what_it_supports: USPS reports nearly 3bn EDDM pieces sent in FY2025, showing the physical channel persists even though it does not prove distributor margins.

sources.title: AI Adoption in the Printing Industry: From Curiosity to Competitive Advantage
sources.what_it_supports: 2025 print-provider survey evidence on AI workflow use, fragmented adoption, barriers and the continued role of human staffing; used as a broad adjacent workflow proxy.

sources.title: Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan
sources.what_it_supports: 2024 U.S. small-business-owner survey on sale, transfer, closure and succession plans; used only as a broad owner-succession proxy.

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: Only routing, targeting, sales support, reporting, administration and some supervision are AI-addressable. The exact industry definition is physical distribution/delivery, so the residual is intentionally assigned a low fraction to represent unlisted field labor.
caveats: The supplied 4-digit 5418 occupation mix is dominated by advertising occupations and is not a physical-distribution occupational census.
The residual's field-labor interpretation is a bounded judgment; a sampling-heavy target may be even less automatable.

role_judgments.rationale: Quotes and proposals automate but local client selling remains human.
This is mainly an agency-proxy role and is assigned a low fraction for a distributor.
Route targeting, coverage analysis and reporting are addressable information tasks.
Scheduling assists, while high-churn crew management and field accountability remain human.
Design support can automate but is peripheral to a physical distributor.
In-person demonstration and sampling cannot be performed by current AI tools.
Administrative selling support automates; client relationship and field coordination persist.
Merchandising/display work is physical and only planning is assistable.
Reporting and campaign planning assist, but this role is a weak proxy for the code.
Creative direction can be augmented but is not core distribution labor.
Treat the residual as door-to-door carriers, drivers, sample distributors, supervisors and physical handling; AI assists dispatch but does not execute delivery.

pi_dist.rationale: Addressable savings sit in rebiddable routing, reporting and administration while USPS EDDM provides a physical-distribution alternative; only a minority is retained by a reliable operator.
caveats: EDDM is an alternative for some, not all, direct-distribution work; the source does not measure private-firm pass-through.

pi_moat.rationale: Route density, local crews and delivery verification can be operationally useful, but the defined service is fundamentally physical and readily comparable on price and coverage.
caveats: No public NAICS-specific retention or switching-cost study was located.

t50_years.rationale: Adjacent print-industry adoption is fragmented; this code has less addressable work because physical delivery remains human, so effective adoption is slower.
caveats: S6 is adjacent printing research, not a 541870 adoption survey.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Set below the print-provider workflow analogue to reflect the high physical-labor share in this exact code.
caveats: Effective adoption excludes generic individual use of AI and does not imply delivery automation.

historical_analogs.value: Route-planning and reporting software are the relevant analogue: they improve coordination around a physical service rather than replace its field labor. Electronic diversion is a separate demand threat.
historical_analogs.rationale: The comparison follows the code's physical-delivery definition and USPS OIG evidence on electronic diversion without claiming a measured 541870 productivity series.
caveats: No historical AI adoption dataset for 541870 was found.

selection.rationale: Use a conservative broad small-business-owner proxy rather than invent an industry-specific age statistic.
caveats: S7 is national small-business evidence, not a representative 541870 owner-age census.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: Broad succession uncertainty is documented, but no reopened source established a NAICS-specific succession shortage, so the boolean remains false.
caveats: False means not documented in the reopened sources, not that succession risk is absent.

active_consolidators.rationale: No verified public source among the reopened materials established an active, NAICS-specific consolidator count; do not infer one from broader print or advertising markets.
caveats: A target search could identify buyers, but that would be new research rather than evidence for this initial count.

buy_mult.rationale: Use a discounted normalized-EBITDA estimate relative to the sold printing owner-earnings/SDE cohort because this labor-heavy, declining-demand physical service has weaker evidence and higher terminal risk.
caveats: S4 is SDE/owner earnings, not EBITDA, and is neither exact NAICS 541870 nor direct-distribution transaction evidence.

exit_mult.rationale: Assume only a small exit improvement if route density, client contracts and compliance are professionalized; no secular multiple expansion is assumed.
caveats: This is an underwriting scenario based on a weak SDE/printing proxy, not a reported exit multiple.

pricing_structure.value: Per-piece or per-route physical-distribution pricing, constrained by rebidding, local labor economics and USPS EDDM for relevant mail alternatives.
pricing_structure.rationale: Census defines physical direct distribution and USPS offers geographic EDDM entry, supporting a competitive-price framework without asserting uniform pricing across all samples and routes.
caveats: EDDM does not cover every non-postal distribution or in-store sampling assignment.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Yes. The low replaceable share is confined to routing, reporting and administration, and the slower adoption path reflects the field-labor core rather than consolidation or terminal demand.

terminal_value.justification: Physical-advertising channels persist, including EDDM, but the long-run electronic-diversion trend and lack of an AI-driven improvement in the core physical service make generalist paid demand structurally vulnerable.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No. The 5418 role mix, print-provider AI survey, printing/SDE valuation cohort and national owner survey are material disclosed proxies; no exact 541870 transaction or adoption series was found.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: AI lowers the back-office layer while physical delivery remains a price-compared service. EDDM and advertiser rebidding create pass-through pressure; delivery reliability is a separate limited moat.

dataset_mismatch.description: The deterministic role mix is 4-digit 5418 advertising data and materially misses the field-distribution workforce that defines 541870.
sensitivity: If more residual wage share is door-to-door or sampling labor, V is lower.
The deterministic 2024-wage/2022-receipts labor-share vintage mismatch remains unchanged.

market_boundary.selection_basis: 2022 NAICS definition; campaign creation/preparation, printing and advertising-specialty distribution are boundary exclusions.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: Changes A, though the low physical-work automation ceiling remains.
No exact-NAICS EBITDA comps were found; M is highly uncertain.
The agency-weighted role proxy can materially overstate or understate the information-layer share.

reviewer_note: All seven driving URLs were reopened on 2026-07-21. The record deliberately does not manufacture a NAICS-specific consolidator count or EBITDA multiple from adjacent sources.
